data.description: A small deciduous tree or large shrub native to Japan, known for its distinctive lobed leaves which are often red, purple, or gold. They are popular in gardens for their beautiful foliage and the sculptural elegance of their fine branching.

careInstructions.light: Partial shade to full sun (especially in cooler climates)
careInstructions.water: Regular watering to keep the soil moist
careInstructions.soil: Well-drained, slightly acidic soil
careInstructions.fertilizer: Balanced, slow-release fertilizer in spring

data.funFact: Japanese Maples are symbols of peace and tranquility in Japanese culture.

similarPlants.name: Siberian Elm
similarPlants.imageAlt: Siberian Elm tree with small, oval leaves.

data.description: Wild Sorrel is known for its bright green, clover-like leaves and tart flavor. It is a widespread weed in temperate areas.

careInstructions.light: Needs partial shade to full sun.
careInstructions.water: Water moderately, allowing soil to dry between waterings.
careInstructions.soil: Prefers well-drained soil rich in organic matter.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Does not require heavy fertilization.

data.funFact: Wild Sorrel is edible and often used in salads for its lemony taste.

data.description: A small tree known for its delicate and colorful foliage, often used in Japanese gardens. Its leaves can vary in color from green to deep red, depending on the variety and season.

careInstructions.light: Prefers partial shade to protect from intense midday sun.
careInstructions.water: Maintain consistently moist, well-drained soil; avoid waterlogged conditions.
careInstructions.soil: Requires acidic, rich, and loamy soil for optimal growth.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Apply a balanced, slow-release fertilizer in spring to support growth.

data.funFact: Japanese Maples are symbols of peace and longevity in Japanese culture.

data.description: A group of small, non-vascular land plants that reproduce via spores rather than seeds.

careInstructions.light: Moss requires a moist environment and thrives in low to medium light conditions.
careInstructions.water: Keep the soil constantly moist with regular watering or in a high humidity area.
careInstructions.soil: Moss grows best in well-drained, acidic soil with a high organic content.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Use a low-nitrogen, slow-release fertilizer sparingly to avoid burning the delicate moss.

data.funFact: Mosses can absorb up to 20 times their weight in water, making them ideal for retaining soil moisture.

careInstructions.water: Regular watering, but not too much.
careInstructions.soil: Well-draining soil.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Fertilize during the growing season.

data.description: Bamboo is a type of evergreen perennial plant that belongs to the grass family. It is known for its fast growth rate and versatility.

careInstructions.light: Bamboo prefers bright, indirect light but can tolerate low light conditions.
careInstructions.water: Bamboo requires consistent moisture, but make sure not to overwater as it can be prone to root rot.
careInstructions.soil: Bamboo prefers well-draining soil that is rich in organic matter.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Bamboo benefits from regular fertilization, especially during the growing season.

data.funFact: Bamboo is one of the fastest-growing plants in the world, with some species growing up to 3 feet per day.

data.description: This is a small tree with a slender trunk and a rounded crown. It has dark green leaves that are oval-shaped and pointed at the tip.

careInstructions.water: Regular watering, but avoid overwatering
careInstructions.soil: Well-draining soil with a slightly acidic pH
careInstructions.fertilizer: Fertilize during the growing season with a balanced fertilizer

data.funFact: This tree is known for its ability to thrive in poor soil conditions.

data.description: Bamboo is a type of evergreen perennial plant that belongs to the grass family. It is known for its fast growth rate and versatility.

careInstructions.light: Bamboo prefers bright, indirect light but can tolerate low light conditions.
careInstructions.water: Bamboo requires consistent moisture, but make sure not to overwater as it can lead to root rot.
careInstructions.soil: Bamboo thrives in well-draining soil with a slightly acidic pH.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Bamboo benefits from regular fertilization during the growing season.

data.funFact: Bamboo is one of the fastest-growing plants in the world, with some species growing up to 3 feet per day.

data.description: Cacti are a family of plants known for their unique ability to store water in their stems, allowing them to thrive in arid environments.

careInstructions.light: Cacti require bright, indirect light but can tolerate some direct sunlight.
careInstructions.water: Water sparingly, allowing the soil to dry out completely between waterings.
careInstructions.soil: Use well-draining soil to prevent waterlogged soil.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Fertilize during the growing season with a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer.

data.funFact: Cacti have been on Earth for over 30 million years.

similarPlants.name: Succulent
similarPlants.imageAlt: A small, round succulent with thick, fleshy leaves.

data.description: Eucalyptus is a popular ornamental plant known for its distinctive blue-green leaves and aromatic scent. It is native to Australia and is often used in floral arrangements and perfumes.

careInstructions.light: Eucalyptus prefers bright, indirect light but can tolerate some direct sunlight.
careInstructions.water: Water Eucalyptus regularly, allowing the soil to dry slightly between waterings.
careInstructions.soil: Use well-draining soil that is rich in organic matter.
careInstructions.fertilizer: Fertilize Eucalyptus during the growing season with a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer.

data.funFact: Eucalyptus is a fast-growing tree that can reach heights of over 100 feet in ideal conditions.

data.description: The Lemon Tree is a popular fruit-bearing tree known for its bright yellow fruit and fragrant flowers. It is commonly grown in warm climates for its edible fruit and ornamental value.

careInstructions.water: Regular watering, but avoid overwatering
careInstructions.soil: Well-draining soil with a pH between 6.0 and 6.5
careInstructions.fertilizer: Fertilize regularly during the growing season

data.funFact: Lemon trees can live for up to 100 years in ideal conditions.

data.description: Monstera deliciosa is a species of flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It is known for its large, glossy leaves with holes and tears that resemble Swiss cheese.

careInstructions.water: Allow the top 1-2 inches of soil to dry out between waterings
careInstructions.soil: Well-draining potting mix
careInstructions.fertilizer: Feed with a balanced fertilizer during the growing season

data.funFact: Monstera deliciosa is also known as the 'Split-Leaf Philodendron' due to its unique leaf shape.
